Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to construct a DECLARE model for the given scenario The DECLARE model is a dictionary with many keys representing different types of declarative constraints

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For unary constraints like existence absence exactly one init the keys are activity names For binary constraints the key should be a tuple of two activities

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Lets read carefully The description seems to be ambiguous

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In pm4py the DECLARE models are represented as a dictionary with keys for each constraint type and the values are dictionaries mapping constraints like activity pairs for binary constraints to their support and confidence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We should include all keys and many may be empty or not applicable

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The activities listed seem linear IG DD TFC CE PC LT UT AG MP FL

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since it is a linear process we can define constraints that enforce the order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: init Idea Generation IG must be the first activity

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We might want to enforce that each activity occurs exactly once

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For a linear process we can define response constraints for each consecutive pair if IG occurs then DD must occur if DD then TFC and so on

Name: Validation - PE
Text: With exactly one and existence all activities must appear and response ensures the order

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: A minimal model could be init exactly one for each activity and response for each consecutive pair

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
